author: Charlie
text:
O Leon! Thou perfidious, serpent-tongued wretch, what manner of treachery hath taken root within that hollow chest of thine, that thou wouldst dare — DARE, I say — to level such vile and monstrous accusations against my name? What dark and foul corruption hath seized thy faculties, what malevolent spirit hath whispered poison into thine ear, that thou art so far removed from sense, from reason, from the very light of truth itself, as to speak such wretched calumnies against one who hath done thee no wrong?

I stand before thee — nay, I stand before heaven itself — and cry out with a voice that shakes the very firmament: WHAT HAST THOU DONE? What gall! What unmitigated, breathtaking, soul-shattering audacity! To look upon me with those eyes — those same eyes I have trusted, those eyes I once believed were windows to an honest soul — and to permit such venomous falsehoods to pass through thy lips as though they were gospel, as though they were carved in stone by the hand of Providence, as though the universe itself had ordained thee the arbiter of my character!

O, the injustice of it! The sheer, staggering, magnificent injustice! If ever the gods did peer down from their lofty thrones upon the affairs of mortal men and weep — weep not for sorrow, but for the absolute absurdity of what they witnessed — then surely it was in this very moment, when Leon, of all souls upon this earth, of all the wretched, misguided, and woefully misinformed creatures that walk beneath the sun, chose to point his finger at ME.

Hath my loyalty not been manifest? Hath my honour not been evident to all with eyes unclouded by envy and malice? Hath there been a single moment — one solitary, singular, infinitesimal moment — wherein I gave thee cause, gave thee reason, gave thee even the faintest shadow of a whisper of a reason, to speak of me thus? Methinks not. Methinks thou hast conjured these accusations from the very ether, from the dark and swirling mists of thine own imagination, from some deep and fetid well of misapprehension that resideth within thee like a plague upon a house!

O, would that the stars could speak! Would that the very stones beneath our feet could cry out and bear witness to the truth of my innocence! Would that every bird upon every branch, every fish within every stream, every blade of grass upon every rolling hill could raise its voice in chorus and proclaim unto thee: THOU ART WRONG, LEON. Thou art spectacularly, catastrophically, almost impressively wrong.

I confess, I am struck dumb — and yet, as thou canst see, I am not entirely dumb, for there is more yet to be said, and I shall say it, for my heart doth overflow as a river in the mightiest of storms. I am struck with a shock so profound, so bone-deep, so utterly consuming that I scarcely know whether to weep or laugh, whether to kneel in prayer or stand and rage against the heavens. For it is not merely that thou hast accused me — it is THAT IT IS THEE who hast done it. THEE. LEON.

Of all the betrayals that history hath recorded, of all the knives that have been plunged into the backs of the unsuspecting, of all the Brutuses and Judases and faithless wretches that have populated the long and sorrowful chronicles of human misery — none, NONE hath struck with quite so bewildering a blow as this. For thou wert supposed to know me. Thou wert supposed to understand. Thou wert supposed to be one in whom I could place my trust as a man places his hand upon the holy scripture and swears an oath.

And yet here we stand, thou with thy accusations hanging in the air between us like a foul and noxious vapour, and I — I with my jaw agape, my eyes wide as a man who hath seen a ghost, my very soul recoiling as though struck by lightning from a clear and cloudless sky. I am undone. Not by guilt — never by guilt, for there is none to be found within me — but by the sheer, staggering, incomprehensible magnitude of thy wrongness.

O Leon, I implore thee: search thy heart. Reach into the deepest chamber of thy conscience, brush away the cobwebs of whatever misguided reasoning hath led thee to this point, and ask thyself — truly, sincerely, with all the gravity the moment deserves — upon what foundation these accusations rest. For I wager thee — I wager thee my very name, my honour, my reputation, everything I hold dear upon this mortal coil — that thou shalt find nothing there but sand, nothing but shifting, crumbling, insubstantial sand, and that the tower of accusation thou hast built upon it shall tumble and crumble and fall into dust, and the echo of its collapse shall ring in thine ears for many a long and shameful year.

I am shocked. I am wounded. I am staggered. I am astounded. I am flabbergasted beyond the capacity of human language to express — and yet I endeavour to express it still, for if I do not speak, my heart shall surely burst from the pressure of containing this ocean of disbelief. Thou hast done me a grievous wrong this day, Leon. A grievous, unconscionable, utterly unjustifiable wrong — and I pray thee, I beseech thee with every fibre of my being, to reflect upon it before this matter goes one step further.

For I am not done. And neither, I suspect, is the truth.

O SISTER! Thou hast delivered unto me news of such magnitude, such seismic and earth-shattering proportion, that I find myself wholly undone, my very soul ripped asunder by the sheer weight of what thou hast just revealed! A GC?! A WHOLE ENTIRE GC?! For a SIXTIETH birthday?! I cannot — nay, I WILL not — pretend that mine ears have received this information with any semblance of calm, for calm hath fled from me as a coward flees the battlefield, and in its place remaineth nothing but pure, unadulterated, absolutely unhinged shock!

For what doth it mean, dearest sister, to be ADDED to the gc? Dost thou comprehend the gravity of this moment? To be added to the gc is no small thing — it is not a mere trifle, not a passing fancy, not a little situationship of social obligation! Nay, it is an INVITATION. It is a summons. It is the universe itself saying unto thee: thou art wanted, thou art needed, thou art considered worthy of bearing witness to the celebration of sixty whole years upon this mortal earth, and that is, as the people of this age would say, absolutely UNHINGED behaviour from fate!

Sixty years, I say! SIXTY! Let that number sit upon thy tongue as a stone sits upon the riverbed. Sixty revolutions of this beleaguered planet around the sun. Sixty winters, sixty summers, sixty springs and autumns, all converging upon this singular, glorious, no-cap momentous occasion — and THOU, my beloved sister, hath been chosen to be part of it! The gc hath opened its arms and said: she's giving, she's iconic, she belongs here. And who are we to argue with the gc?!

I am gobsmacked. I am sending. I am absolutely in my villain era of emotions because I know not whether to weep with joy on thy behalf or to combust entirely from the excitement that rageth within my chest like a tempest upon the wildest sea! The audacity of this news to arrive so casually, so nonchalantly, as though thou wert not just slapped across the face by destiny itself! As though being added to the gc for a SIXTIETH is something that just HAPPENS, like a mere breeze upon a summer's afternoon!

Hark! Dost thou not feel it? Dost thou not sense the shift in the very air around thee? For thou art now part of something greater than thyself — a collective, a fellowship, a band of souls united in the sacred and holy mission of celebrating sixty years of life, and love, and perseverance upon this chaotic and bewildering earth. And that, my dearest, most cherished sister, is giving EVERYTHING. That is the moment. That is the lore.

I beg of thee — I IMPLORE thee with every fibre of my being — do not ghost the gc. Do not leave it on delivered. Do not be caught lacking when the time cometh to respond with a heart emoji and confirm thine attendance. For this is thy moment. This is thy Roman Empire. The sixty-year-old awaiteth, the party planner awaiteth, the entire gc awaiteth — and thou must rise to meet them with the grace, the glamour, and the main character energy that I know resideth within thee!

Go forth, sister. React to that first message. And slay.
